rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive linear dimerization of electron-deficient vinylarenes to 1,4-diarylbutanes in an undivided cell with carbon electrodes using n-Bu4NBr/CH3CN-DMF, where CH3CN serves as the H-atom source.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of the electron-deficient vinylarene generates a radical anion that adds to a second vinylarene; after H-atom abstraction from CH3CN the resulting carbanion is re-oxidized at the anode to a radical, which abstracts a second H from CH3CN to give the saturated dimer.

key_intermediates.name: distonic radical anion from radical addition to second vinylarene (16a)
benzylic carbanion after H-atom transfer from CH3CN (17a)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces vinylarene 6a to radical anion 15a, which adds across another 6a to give distonic intermediate 16a; HAT from CH3CN furnishes carbanion 17a, which is oxidized at the anode to radical 18a; a second HAT from CH3CN delivers the 1,4-diarylbutane 12a.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 12a not detected under standard conditions with 2.0 equiv TEMPO, consistent with a radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of D2O (0.1 mL) gave 12a in 62% yield with no deuterium incorporation, indicating H does not come from water.

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing CH3CN with CD3CN gave [D]-12a in 60% yield with 100% D incorporation at the benzylic positions, demonstrating CH3CN is the H-atom source.

notes: Authors note that other mechanistic pathways cannot be ruled out. Strong electron-withdrawing substituents favor reaction by stabilizing the intermediate anion 17a, consistent with the proposed cathodic reduction initiation. A cross-coupling variant (4-vinylpyridine 6y + acrylate 6aa → 12aa, 52%) is also demonstrated.
